executive_summary: Canada is experiencing an unprecedented ransomware surge with 99 organizations publicly listed as victims on June 13, 2026—the highest concentration ever observed in a single day. High-profile targets include Canada Goose (retail), National Money Mart (financial services with database leaked), Peterborough Public Health, Eastern Townships School Board, and the Municipality of North Perth. The Qilin ransomware group leads with 18 victims, followed by SafePay (13), Play (11), and Clop (11). This coordinated wave spans critical sectors including healthcare, education, public sector, financial services, and infrastructure—signaling either a mass exploitation campaign or delayed disclosure of a widespread supply-chain compromise.

canada_landscape.summary: Canada experienced an extreme ransomware event on June 13, 2026, with 99 organizations publicly listed as victims across all provinces and sectors. Critical infrastructure including healthcare (Peterborough Public Health, multiple medical facilities), education (Eastern Townships School Board, Teccart college), telecommunications (Eeyou Communications Network), and municipal government (Municipality of North Perth, MRC de Maskinongé) were compromised. No CCCS advisories were identified in this reporting period.
canada_landscape.retailers: Canada Goose (luxury retail) claimed by CoinbaseCartel ransomware group; Active Green + Ross (automotive retail) claimed by Sinobi
canada_landscape.financial: National Money Mart Company breached by Everest ransomware with database leaked; Morison Insurance Brokers claimed by Sinobi; Trucash (financial services) claimed by Lynx; multiple accounting firms (Pewarchuk CPA, MG Chartered Professional Accountant) and credit unions (Axion50plus) compromised

report_2w.executive_summary: Multiple critical vulnerabilities are being actively exploited in the wild, with the most urgent being CVE-2026-50751, a Check Point VPN authentication bypass that Qilin ransomware is actively exploiting with no credentials required. CISA has added Cisco, Chrome, and Arista flaws to its Known Exploited Vulnerabilities catalog due to active exploitation. A major supply chain attack called Shai-Hulud has compromised over 100 NPM and PyPI packages through self-propagating worms named Miasma and Hades. Canada faces potential privacy challenges as Signal and DuckDuckGo consider exiting the Canadian market over a proposed lawful access bill, while the government plans a social media ban for children under 16.

canada_landscape.summary: Canada faces significant policy developments affecting cybersecurity and privacy. Signal and DuckDuckGo are weighing exit from Canada over a proposed lawful access bill requiring message and photo filtering, raising encryption concerns among CISOs. The government is also preparing an online harms bill including a social media ban for children under 16. FIFA World Cup 2026 scams are already active with fake domains and phishing sites targeting Canadian visitors.
